LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT KRISTIAN LUNDGREN-KOSZEGHY, DMD, MMSC'S MOTION TO BIFURICATE ISSUES IS GRANTED IN PART. PURSUANT TO CCP 597.5, THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS DEFENSE MUST BE TRIED "SEPARATELY AND BEFORE ANY OTHER ISSUES IN THE CASE ARE TRIED." THE COURT DENIES WITHOUT PREJUDICE THE REQUEST FOR SEPARATE JURIES. DEFENDANT HAS NOT SHOWN THAT SEPARATE JURIES WILL FURTHER JUDICIAL ECONOMY. THIS ORDER IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO RAISING THE ISSUE BEFORE THE TRIAL JUDGE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT KRISTIAN LUNDGREN-KOSZEGHY, D.M.D, M.M.S.C'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT IS NOT ENTITLED TO SUMMARY JUDGMENT. AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PROFESSIONAL NEGLIGENCE, THE CAUSE OF ACTION IS NOT BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS PER CCP 340.6. THE OPERATIVE DATE FOR THE RUNNING OF THE ONE YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS WAS THE DISCOVERY OF HER INJURY ON APRIL 20, 2011. (SEE POINTS & AUTH., P. 7:18-20; OPP., 6:25-26.) PLAINTIFF SERVED HER NOTICE OF INTENTION TO COMMENCE THE ACTION ON MARCH 30, 2012, AND THE TIME FOR COMMENCEMENT OF THE ACTION WAS EXTENDED BY 90 DAYS TO JUNE 30, 2012. (CCP 364(D). SEE SEROVAYSKAYA DECL., 27, EXHIBIT H.) PLAINTIFF TIMELY FILED HER COMPLAINT ON JUNE 28, 2012. (UMF NO.1.) AS TO WHETHER DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT FELL WITHIN THE COMMUNITY STANDARD OF CARE, PLAINTIFF HAS COME FORWARD WITH CONFLICTING EXPERT EVIDENCE IN THE DECLARATION OF DON MISSIRLIAN, D.D.S., AND DEFENDANT IS NOT ENTITLED TO SUMMARY JUDGMENT. (MUNRO V. REGENTS OF UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA (1989) 215 CAL.APP.3D 977, 984-85.) AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONCEALMENT, DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENT IS PREDICATED ON PREVAILING ON THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, AND THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Sep-25-2013 Text Ruling

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Bifuricate Issues
Off Calendar Aug-28-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested, as modified. DEFENDANT KRISTIAN LUNDGREN-KOSZEGHY, DMD, MMSC's Motion To Bifuricate Issues is granted in part. Pursuant to CCP 597.5, the statute of limitations defense must be tried "separately and before any other issues in the case are tried." The Court denies without prejudice the request for separate juries. DEFENDANT has not shown that separate juries will further judicial economy. This order is without prejudice to raising the issue before the trial judge. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - VALERYA SERVAYSKAYA, TEL. NO. 424-6652, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT KRISTIAN LUNDGREN-KOSZEGHY, D.M.D, M.M.S.C'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is Denied. Defendant is not entitled to summary judgment. As to the first cause of action for professional negligence, the cause of action is not barred by the statute of limitations per CCP 340.6. The operative date for the running of the one year statute of limitations was the discovery of her injury on April 20, 2011. (See Points & Auth., p. 7:18-20; Opp., 6:25-26.) Plaintiff served her notice of intention to commence the action on March 30, 2012, and the time for commencement of the action was extended by 90 days to June 30, 2012. (CCP 364(d). See Serovayskaya Decl., 27, Exhibit H.) Plaintiff timely filed her Complaint on June 28, 2012. (UMF no.1.) As to whether Defendant's conduct fell within the community standard of care, Plaintiff has come forward with conflicting expert evidence in the declaration of Don Missirlian, D.D.S., and Defendant is not entitled to summary judgment. (Munro v. Regents of University of California (1989) 215 Cal.App.3d 977, 984-85.) As to the second cause of action for concealment, Defendant's argument is predicated on prevailing on the first cause of action, and the motion for summary judgment is denied. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM
=(302/MJM)

The Court did not receive a courtesy copy of the papers-including a proof of service-in compliance with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. In addition, this matter should be calendared in Department 301 (the discovery calendar) at 9:00 a.m. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
